label: Sports Coordinator (i9 Sports) - entry-level coaching / operations vs MLB-focused candidate

profile.profile_text:
First Name	Aiden
Last Name	Ginsberg
Email	aideng04@gmail.com
Current Status	Recent Graduate (0-12 months)
Job Type Preference	Full Time Role
3. What types of roles are you targeting right now?	I want a job with the MLB and I am willing to take any position that will open the door for me and allow me to prove myself. I am willing to work hard and put in my dues. My ultimate goal in 5-10 years is to grow in a front office role but I understand I have to start somewhere. I do have a lot of fan/guest/event/logistic sports experience so that may be a logicial entry point for me.
Secondary or adjacent roles you would consider	I will consider any opportunity to get my foot in the door.
Consumer, Retail & Lifestyle	Sports and Entertainment
Specific companies you are particularly interested in (max 10)	Any MLB team (Yankees, Marlins, Rays). Secondary preference would be an NFL team than a NBA team
5. Are there any roles or industries you D O N O T want?	I only want to work for a professional sports organization
How open are you to non-obvious entry points?	Very open
6. Location preferences	Anywhere in the US - I will relocate anywhere
7. Timeline for starting work	Immediate
8. What do you believe are your strongest skills right now?	I have very strong people/fan facing skills. I am very charismatic and understand the value of great service. I also understand baseball inside and out.
9. What about your job search gives you most concern?	MLB is so super competitive
10. How do you prefer feedback delivered?	Direct & Blunt (don't sugarcoat it)
Resume Paste	AIDEN GINSBERG (561)789-9145 | aideng04@gmail.com | LinkedIn Profile PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY Entry-level game day operations and guest experience professional with Division I athletics and championship event experience. Proven ability to support high-volume live events, coordinate logistics, and deliver consistent fan and staff experiences in fast-paced environments. Seeking an entry-level role in guest experience, event operations, or fan engagement where I can support game day execution, staff coordination, and fan-facing operations. EDUCATION Florida State University – Tallahassee, FL Bachelor of Science in Sport Management GPA: 3.41 Dec 2025 Awards/Honors: Dean’s List (Fall 2023, Spring 2024, Fall 2024, Spring 2025)| Full Tuition Bright Futures Scholarship Global Sports Management in Spain Study Abroad – Valencia, Madrid, and Barcelona, Spain Summer 2024 CORE COMPETENCIES Game Day & Event Operations | Guest Experience & Fan Services | Staff & Volunteer Coordination | Live Event Logistics & Setup | Corporate Hospitality & Sponsor Activation | Player & Team Support (Non-Playing) | Administrative & Front Office Support | Customer Service & Issue Resolution RELEVANT EXPERIENCE Game Day Representative (FSU Football) | Legends & ASM Global – Tallahassee, FL Aug 2025 - Nov 2025 Executed game day operations for Division I football events serving 60,000+ fans, supporting sponsor activations, corporate hospitality, and premium guest experiences Coordinated vendor and sponsor load-ins, tracked marketing assets, and ensured setup and breakdown of hospitality spaces Served as on-site point of contact for guest issues, sponsor needs, and last-minute operational changes during live events Event Operations Volunteer | NCAA Men’s College World Series – Omaha, NE June 2025 Selected to support on-site event operations for one of collegiate baseball’s premier championships, assisting with venue logistics, fan services, and game day execution across multiple event days Supported crowd flow, guest assistance, and operational setup, gaining exposure to large-scale baseball event operations Events & Facilities Staff | Florida State University Athletics – Tallahassee, FL Sep 2023 – Dec 2023 Supported football and baseball game day operations by preparing venues, managing signage and equipment setup, and ensuring facilities met safety and operational standards Provided on-field and behind-the-scenes support during football games, coordinating with media, operations staff, and event personnel to ensure smooth, on-time execution League Coordinator Intern | Caribbean Baseball Organization – Miami, FL Feb 2024 – Aug 2024 Led multi-channel recruitment outreach (calls, emails, texts) to high school seniors and college athletes, supporting roster development for summer league operations Served as primary contact for players and families, answering logistical questions and supporting enrollment and onboarding processes Assisted with league logistics, scheduling coordination, and day-to-day operational support to expand program reach Assistant Basketball Coach | Reach One Youth Organization, Inc – Tallahassee, FL Jan 2024 - May 2025 Led weekly practices and skill development sessions for 60+ youth athletes (ages 10–14), emphasizing teamwork, discipline and accountability Served as a mentor and point of support for athletes and families, reinforcing communication and leadership skills ADDITIONAL EXPERIENCE Social Media Content Creator & Coordinator Intern | Workforce Ready Now – Boca Raton, FL May 2025 - Current Conducted market and competitor research and produced short-form Instagram and TikTok content using Canva to support Gen Z audience engagement Kitchen Steward | Delta Gamma Sorority – Tallahassee, FL Aug 2024 - Current Supported meal prep, dining room setup for 300+ people, and kitchen operations, collaborating with chef and house manager to ensure efficient service Recruitment Coordinator | The Placement Gurus – Boca Raton, FL May 2023 – Aug 2023 Managed job postings, candidate screening, and PCRecruiter database updates to support end-to-end hiring operations INVOLVEMENT & VOLUNTEERISM Member, FSU Sport Management Society | Sigma Alpha Epsilon Fraternity
Cover Letter or Other Writing Samples	AIDEN GINSBERG (561)789-9145 | aideng04@gmail.com | LinkedIn Profile September 25, 2025 Hiring Committee Cincinnati Reds Re: Application for the Special Event Sales and Service Coordinator Dear Hiring Team, Baseball has been my north star since I was a kid, and I’ve built my education and experience around one goal: earning my way into the business side of the game. When I saw the posting for Special Event Sales and Service Coordinator with the Reds, I knew it was the exact role I’ve been accelerating toward, literally, since I’m graduating a semester early from Florida State to be ready for the 2026 season. What sets me apart isn’t just my degree in Sport Management. It’s the mix of sales grit, event operations experience, and genuine people skills I bring to the table. At the Caribbean Baseball Organization, I built a recruiting pipeline through calls, emails, and outreach, developing persistence and relationship-building skills that drive results. At the College World Series, I supported game-day operations on one of the sport’s biggest stages, handling logistics and fan experience where details mattered and pressure was high. Across my roles in event coordination, game-day support, and youth coaching, I’ve learned how to connect with people, whether it’s a fan, a client, or a 12-year-old athlete who just needs someone to believe in them. The Reds are looking for someone who can sell events, coordinate logistics, and deliver a best-in-class client experience. That’s exactly the mix of skills I’ve built, backed by the kind of drive and adaptability that can’t be taught. I’m coachable, competitive, and committed to bringing the same energy to your special events team that I’ve brought to every field, venue, and locker room I’ve worked in. And if reading cover letters isn’t your favorite part of the day, feel free to take a break and check out this short video bio I made to introduce myself: Aiden Ginsberg Intro I’d love the chance to bring my hustle, creativity, and love for the game to Great American Ball Park. Thank you for considering me. I graduate in early December and I’ll be ready to get to work the very next day. Sincerely, Aiden Ginsberg AIDEN GINSBERG (561)789-9145 | aideng04@gmail.com | LinkedIn Profile October 27, 2025 Hiring Team Texas Rangers Baseball Foundation Arlington, TX Dear Hiring Team, I am writing to express my interest in the Foundation Intern position with the Texas Rangers Baseball Foundation. As a lifelong baseball fan and Sport Management major at Florida State University, I am passionate about the ways this game connects communities, inspires fans, and creates lasting impact beyond the field. Throughout my time at FSU, I have built hands-on experience across game day operations, fan engagement, and community events from supporting FSU Football as part of Legends and ASM Global to volunteering at the NCAA Men’s College World Series and coordinating outreach for the Caribbean Baseball Organization. These experiences taught me how to stay organized, communicate with diverse groups, and thrive in fast paced, team driven environments. I am eager to bring that same energy and reliability to the Texas Rangers Foundation. Whether it is assisting with fundraising events, managing the Foundation kiosk, or handling the day to day administrative tasks that keep operations running smoothly, I am ready to contribute wherever needed. I take pride in following through, working hard, and staying positive, especially when representing an organization that gives back to its community. Thank you for your time and consideration. I have attached my resume and would welcome the chance to discuss opportunities to support your team. And if cover letters start to feel repetitive, I’d invite you to check out a quick video I made to introduce myself: Aiden Ginsberg Intro Sincerely, Aiden Ginsberg
13. Anything else we should know about your situation?	no constraints - will even entertain P/T or internships if it means getting a foot in the door

jobText:
Job highlights
Identified by Google from the original job post
Qualifications
Excellent communication skills
Sport-specific coaching experience & knowledge
Highly motivated self-starter; can work independently & solve problems
Awareness & ability to take charge of any situation to ensure the safety of players
Positive attitude and a strong ability to build professional relationships
Ability to work a varied work schedule including weekends and some evenings (for the full duration of the season, ~7 weeks)
Benefits
A team-based atmosphere with a focus on Fun!
Be a role model for athletes
Online training opportunities
Compensation: $15.00 - $18.00 per hour
Responsibilities
The Sports Coordinator is responsible for enhancing the i9 Sports Experience for players and coaches
The Sports Coordinator will need to build relationships with players, parents, and coaches while making sure that the instructional aspects of the i9 Sports Experience are delivered
Observing, assessing, and assisting our coaches is paramount
The position entails hands-on involvement with practices and games among all age groups and skill levels
Empower volunteer coaches to lead practice sessions focusing on fundamentals, skill development, and fun
Teach & demonstrate core concepts including Sportsmanship values
Supervise the overall operation of designated sport on game day
Consistently demonstrate a positive attitude and superior customer service skills
